data.narrative:
Rigetti Computing is a public-stage quantum computing company with a current valuation of $5.5B. The peer universe comprises 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: IonQ ($11.0B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), Infleqtion ($1.8B), and Archer Materials ($60M), representing a broad dispersion across the emerging quantum computing public market.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $1.9B. This median sits at the midpoint of a wide peer range ($60M to $11.0B), reflecting material heterogeneity in how the public markets value quantum computing firms at present. The method supports a valuation corridor of $1.6B (low) to $2.2B (high), derived around the $1.9B median.

Rigetti's current $5.5B valuation sits substantially above the peer-median implied range, a discrepancy likely attributable to company-specific factors not captured in sector-wide comparables—including capital raised ($300M+), product differentiation, or near-term commercialization prospects. The wide dispersion in peer valuations ($60M to $11.0B) signals that stage and sector alone are weak valuation anchors; material uncertainty persists around which quantum computing platforms will achieve durable market adoption. A single-method triangulation limits confidence; additional revenue multiple or DCF analysis would strengthen the assessment.
